original_hypothesis.title: disableAutoVoting Permanent Lockout When AVM Has Already Voted in Current Epoch
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: disableAutoVoting calls IVoter(voter).reset(tokenId) which has the onlyNewEpoch modifier. If the AVM has already voted for this tokenId in the current epoch, lastVoted[tokenId] >= epochStart(now), causing reset to revert with 'VOTED'. Additionally, the voted flag prevents transferFrom. Users cannot exit AVM until the next epoch, and with WEEK=1800, the next epoch window is the 1200-second non-last-hour window of the next 30-minute epoch.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The AVM votes in the last hour of each epoch via performLockAction. After voting, the user cannot disable auto-voting until the next epoch. With short epochs, this creates a tight window where users can exit, and if they miss it, they're locked for another full epoch.

proof_of_signal.pattern: disableAutoVoting calls IVoter(voter).reset(tokenId) which has onlyNewEpoch modifier checking lastVoted[tokenId]. AVM sets lastVoted when voting via voteLocks -> voter.vote.

preconditions: User has enabled auto-voting for their tokenId
AVM executor has already called performLockAction with isVote=true for this epoch
User attempts to disable auto-voting in the same epoch

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: lastVoted[tokenId] = epochStart(now) + 1 after AVM votes. onlyNewEpoch requires epochStart(now) > lastVoted. Until next epoch starts, this check fails.
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Not an attacker-driven exploit, but a design flaw that traps users. Malicious executor could vote early in the 'last hour' window to maximize lockout duration.
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Locked users cannot participate in alternative voting strategies or exit during volatile market conditions

economic_questions: What is the maximum time a user can be locked in AVM?
Can the executor strategically time votes to maximize user lockout?
Is there any emergency exit mechanism?
validation_requirements: Confirm disableAutoVoting reverts after AVM has voted in same epoch
Calculate lockout duration: from AVM vote time to next epoch's non-last-hour window
Verify no alternative exit path exists

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Indirect: user cannot react to market conditions
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Up to ~1800 seconds (one epoch) per lockout

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Affects users every epoch after AVM votes
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Reduced user flexibility in managing veNFT positions

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Temporary loss of control over voting position

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm onlyNewEpoch blocks reset after AVM vote

validation_result.mechanism: onlyNewEpoch blocks disableAutoVoting after AVM vote
validation_result.impact_summary: Users cannot exit AVM (disable auto-voting) during the same epoch in which the AVM executor has already voted their tokenId, temporarily locking them into the AVM for up to ~600 seconds with WEEK=1800.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The disableAutoVoting function in AutoVotingEscrowManager calls IVoter(voter).reset(tokenId) which has the onlyNewEpoch modifier. When the AVM executor calls performLockAction with isVote=true, the underlying voteLocks function calls voter.vote() which sets lastVoted[tokenId] = epochStart(block.timestamp) + 1. Subsequently, onlyNewEpoch checks 'epochStart(block.timestamp) <= lastVoted[tokenId]', which evaluates to 'epochStart(now) <= epochStart(now) + 1' = true, causing the reset call to revert with 'VOTED'. Since disableAutoVoting has no alternative path and reset is mandatory before the transfer (because voted[tokenId] is true, blocking transferFrom), users are completely locked out of exiting AVM until the next epoch's valid voting window opens.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Direct code evidence confirms the defect. The onlyNewEpoch modifier at VoterV3.sol blocks reset after vote in same epoch. disableAutoVoting at AutoVotingEscrowManager.sol:85 unconditionally calls reset. No fallback or emergency exit mechanism exists.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: reset(tokenId) calls into VoterV3 which has onlyNewEpoch modifier. After AVM votes the token in the current epoch, lastVoted[tokenId] = epochStart(now)+1, causing onlyNewEpoch to revert with 'VOTED'. No alternative path exists to exit AVM.
This modifier blocks reset when epochStart(now) <= lastVoted[tokenId]. After vote() sets lastVoted to epochStart(now)+1, this condition is true for the entire remaining epoch.
Sets lastVoted[tokenId] = epochStart(now) + 1 which triggers the onlyNewEpoch blocker for any subsequent reset call in the same epoch.
Calls voter.vote for each token, which sets lastVoted, preventing the token from being reset in the same epoch.

calling_sequence: 1. Executor calls performLockAction(avmIndex, start, end, isVote=true) during last hour
3. For each active lock, _vote() calls IVoter(voter).vote(tokenId, pools, weights)
5. User calls disableAutoVoting(tokenId) later in same epoch (after last hour)
8. VoterV3.reset has onlyNewEpoch modifier which checks: epochStart(block.timestamp) <= lastVoted[tokenId]
9. epochStart(now) <= epochStart(now) + 1 is TRUE → reverts with 'VOTED'
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The critical path is: performLockAction → voteLocks → voter.vote (sets lastVoted) → then later user → disableAutoVoting → voter.reset → onlyNewEpoch reverts. The onlyNewEpoch modifier at VoterV3.sol:220 checks 'epochStart(block.timestamp) <= lastVoted[_tokenId]'. Since vote() at VoterV3.sol:193 sets lastVoted[_tokenId] = epochStart(block.timestamp) + 1, the condition epochStart(now) <= epochStart(now)+1 is always true within the same epoch, blocking reset and therefore blocking disableAutoVoting.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that contradict the hypothesis. WEEK=1800 is hardcoded in BlackTimeLibrary.sol.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Scenario: (1) User enables auto-voting for tokenId. (2) Executor calls performLockAction with isVote=true during the 'last hour' window [epochStart+1500, epochStart+1800). (3) voter.vote sets lastVoted[tokenId] = epochStart+1. (4) User wants to exit AVM, calls disableAutoVoting. (5) disableAutoVoting first checks !isLastHour - if still in last hour, it reverts with 'LH'. (6) If past last hour (new epoch started), the onlyNewEpoch check in the new epoch's first 300 seconds would revert with 'DW'. (7) After 300 seconds into new epoch, onlyNewEpoch passes because epochStart(now) for the new epoch > lastVoted from old epoch. So the lockout is from the AVM vote time until the next epoch's vote start (epochStart + 300). Maximum lockout with WEEK=1800: ~600 seconds. This is confirmed by the code.
validation_result.impact_assessment: MEDIUM severity. All AVM-enabled veNFT holders are affected every epoch after the AVM executor votes. Users temporarily lose the ability to exit AVM and regain control of their veNFT position. With WEEK=1800, the lockout window is at most ~600 seconds (10 minutes). With a more standard WEEK value (7 days), the lockout could extend up to almost a full week, making this significantly more impactful. The defect prevents emergency exits during volatile market conditions when users may urgently need to change their voting strategy or transfer their veNFT.
recommended_mitigations: Add a specialized reset path in VoterV3 callable only by the AVM manager that bypasses the onlyNewEpoch check, or add an overload of reset that skips the modifier when called by AVM
Move the voter.reset() call before releaseLock and handle it conditionally - if the token hasn't voted this epoch, call reset; if it has, skip reset since the votes will naturally expire at epoch end
Add an emergency exit function in AutoVotingEscrowManager that can be called without reset, accepting that the vote weights remain until epoch end
Have disableAutoVoting check if the token has already voted this epoch and defer the actual exit to the next epoch via a pending-exit queue
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy VotingEscrow, VoterV3, AutoVotingEscrowManager, AutoVotingEscrow, and strategy contracts. 2. Create a veNFT lock. 3. Enable auto-voting for the token. 4. Advance time to the 'last hour' window. 5. As executor, call performLockAction with isVote=true. 6. Advance time past the last hour but still in the same epoch. 7. As user, call disableAutoVoting - expect revert with 'VOTED'. 8. Advance time to the next epoch + 300 seconds. 9. Call disableAutoVoting again - expect success.
uncertainties: The actual WEEK value in production deployment could differ from the 1800 seconds hardcoded in BlackTimeLibrary - a standard 7-day week would make this issue much more severe
It's unclear if there are any off-chain mechanisms (like the executor helping users exit) that could mitigate this in practice
additional_testing_needed: Verify the exact lockout duration with different WEEK values
Test if releaseLock's state changes (removing from locks array, approving manager) cause any issues when disableAutoVoting reverts and these changes are rolled back
Verify that the voted flag on VotingEscrow also blocks transferFrom, confirming there's no alternative exit path
